title: Diuris blakneyae
taxonomy: Diuris blakneyae is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It belongs to the genus Diuris, which includes several species of donkey orchids native to Australia. Further classification places Diuris blakneyae within the subgenus Diuris, which contains species characterized by their distinctive donkey-like flower shape. The species is also classified as Diuris blakneyae subsp. blakneyae, with no recognized subspecies at this time. This classification reflects the unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships of Diuris blakneyae within the orchid family.

parts: Diuris blakneyae, commonly known as the 'Blakney's donkey orchid' is an orchid species native to Western Australia. intro: Diuris blakneyae is a species of orchid native to Western Australia, commonly known as the Blakney's donkey orchid. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
